highlights.title: Account mode
highlights.description: an org-scoped API key (no `MUSHI_PROJECT_ID` required) now

highlights.title: stdio fix
highlights.description: all logger and `console.*` output is routed to stderr so stdout

highlights.title: Web
highlights.description: Pointer Events drag with tap-vs-drag threshold (6 px), safe-area clamping, optional edge-snap on release, per-project `localStorage` persistence, arrow-key nudge for keyboard accessibility. New `draggable?: boolean | { persist?, snapToEdge?, axis? }` config type in `@mushi-mushi/core`.

highlights.title: React Native
highlights.description: `PanResponder`-based drag + `AsyncStorage` persistence + safe-area clamping.

highlights.title: Flutter
highlights.description: Long-press-to-drag via `GestureDetector` + `SharedPreferences` persistence + edge-snap.

highlights.title: iOS
highlights.description: `UIPanGestureRecognizer` via `MushiFabDragController` + `UserDefaults` persistence + animated edge-snap.

highlights.title: Android
highlights.description: `OnTouchListener` tap-vs-drag + `SharedPreferences` persistence + optional edge-snap. New `DraggableConfig` data class.

highlights.title: All platforms
highlights.description: New `theme: 'inherit'` resolves the host app's dark mode at runtime (`prefers-color-scheme` / `color-scheme` on web, `traitCollection.userInterfaceStyle` on iOS, `UiModeManager` on Android, `Brightness` on Flutter). New `accent` + `accentText` config tokens for brand-color override.

highlights.title: Web
highlights.description: Parameterized `getWidgetStyles(theme, accent)` with `widgetAccent` / `widgetAccentWash` / `widgetAccentInk` tokens; fixed undefined `var(--mushi-text-dim)` references; WCAG AA re-verified in both themes.

highlights.title: React Native / Android
highlights.description: Explicit disabled-state colors (no more white-on-white disabled buttons).

highlights.title: Web
highlights.description: `visualViewport` manager on `open()`; lifts panel above mobile keyboard; scrolls focused `textarea` / `input` into view; `100dvh` bottom-sheet layout on narrow viewports; torn down on `close()` / `destroy()`.

highlights.title: React Native
highlights.description: `KeyboardAvoidingView` `behavior="height"` (Android) + `ScrollView(keyboardShouldPersistTaps)`.

highlights.title: Flutter
highlights.description: `SingleChildScrollView` + `MediaQuery.viewInsets.bottom` inset.

highlights.title: iOS
highlights.description: `keyboardWillShow` / `keyboardWillHide` observers + `CGAffineTransform` lift.

highlights.title: New:
highlights.description: `checkReportPayloadSize`, `estimateJsonBytes`, `formatBytes`, and `MAX_REPORT_PAYLOAD_BYTES` payload-guard helpers. `checkReportPayloadSize` serializes once and reports a distinct `serializeFailed` outcome (e.g. circular references in metadata) instead of a generic "too large".

highlights.title: Hardening:
highlights.description: the offline retry queue now treats `PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE` and `SERIALIZE_FAILED` as permanent failures, so a single oversized report can no longer poison-pill the queue and block every later report.

highlights.title: New — session replay:
highlights.description: opt-in `capture.replay: 'rrweb' | 'lite' | 'sentry' | 'off'` records a rolling buffer that attaches to the report on submit. rrweb recording masks all text and inputs by default (`maskAllText` + `maskAllInputs`), records continuously from init, and trims the buffer while preserving the most recent full snapshot so replays stay playable. `'off'` is the default.

highlights.title: New — screenshot annotation:
highlights.description: the report panel's "Mark up" button opens an interactive overlay (highlight / blur / arrow) so reporters can circle the problem and redact sensitive regions before submitting. The annotation session stays open until the reporter confirms with "Done"; touch strokes now resolve correctly on lift (`touchend`).

highlights.title: New — screenshot compression
highlights.description: before upload to keep payloads small.

highlights.title: Hardening:
highlights.description: oversized reports are progressively degraded — drop the replay buffer, then the screenshot — before being dropped entirely with a `report:failed` event, so the offline queue is never wedged. The replay capture is now torn down on `Mushi.destroy()` (no observer/listener leak) and guarded against an init/`updateConfig` race that could install a stale capture.

highlights.title: New:
highlights.description: `checkReportPayloadSize`, `estimateJsonBytes`, `formatBytes`, and `MAX_REPORT_PAYLOAD_BYTES` payload-guard helpers. `checkReportPayloadSize` serializes once and reports a distinct `serializeFailed` outcome (e.g. circular references in metadata) instead of a generic "too large".

highlights.title: New — screenshot annotation:
highlights.description: the report panel's "Mark up" button opens an interactive overlay (highlight / blur / arrow) so reporters can circle the problem and redact sensitive regions before submitting. The annotation session stays open until the reporter confirms with "Done"; touch strokes now resolve correctly on lift (`touchend`).

highlights.title: New — screenshot compression
highlights.description: before upload to keep payloads small.

highlights.title: Hardening:
highlights.description: oversized reports are progressively degraded — drop the replay buffer, then the screenshot — before being dropped entirely with a `report:failed` event, so the offline queue is never wedged. The replay capture is now torn down on `Mushi.destroy()` (no observer/listener leak) and guarded against an init/`updateConfig` race that could install a stale capture.

highlights.title: Layout changed from diamond to horizontal row
highlights.description: (Plan → Do → Check → Act) for left-to-right reading flow.

highlights.title: Act→Plan loop-back arc
highlights.description: hand-crafted cubic-bezier `M act.bottom C act.bottom+120 plan.bottom+120 plan.bottom` so the return path sweeps 120 px below the node row rather than producing a flat line (the previous `getBezierPath` Bottom→Bottom path with equal source/target Y resolved to zero curvature).

highlights.title: Arrow opacity
highlights.description: all edge main-stroke paths are now `opacity: 1` unconditionally — inactive edges were previously rendered at 88 % which read as transparent at fitView zoom. Track layer raised from 12 % to 22 %, glow layer from 13 % to 22 %.

highlights.title: Auto-fit on init
highlights.description: added `onInit` callback that defers `rf.fitView()` one animation frame. The `fitView` prop fires before edge paths are measured, so without this the loop arc's bounding box was excluded and the view was clipped on first load.

highlights.title: Canvas height
highlights.description: raised from 320 → 380 px (live variant) to accommodate the loop arc.

highlights.title: Minimap removed
highlights.description: — unnecessary for a fixed 4-node diagram; reclaims ~88 px of vertical canvas.

highlights.title: X-axis
highlights.description: 5 evenly-spaced date tick labels (was only first + last).

highlights.title: Y-axis
highlights.description: max + midpoint + 0 reference lines (was max + 0 only).

highlights.title: Value labels
highlights.description: suppressed when bar < 20 % of max to prevent overlap on dense data.

highlights.title: Ghost bars
highlights.description: zero-report days now show a 4 px dashed placeholder so the grid is visually consistent.

highlights.title: Chart height
highlights.description: raised from `h-24` (96 px) to `h-[7rem]` (112 px) for better vertical resolution.

highlights.title: Segment min-height
highlights.description: each severity span gets `minHeight: 2px` so a 1-report slice of 100-report day is always visible.

highlights.title: Rewards subsystem
highlights.description: (`src/rewards.ts`): full tester-rewards API — `initRewards`, `updateRewardsUser`, `getTier`, `enqueueActivity`. Connects to `/v1/rewards/*` backend endpoints.

highlights.title: Proactive triggers
highlights.description: (`src/proactive-triggers.ts`): schedule-based and event-based triggers that surface the widget at the right moment without developer code.

highlights.title: `pulseTrigger()`
highlights.description: widget-level pulse animation accessible from `Mushi.pulseTrigger()`.

highlights.title: Screenshot simplification
highlights.description: `screenshot.take()` now returns `Promise<string | null>` — callers no longer need to check an `{ ok, dataUrl }` result object.

highlights.title: `beforeSendFeedback` + `onCrashedLastRun`
highlights.description: lifecycle hooks wired into the init flow.

highlights.title: `mushi project create`
highlights.description: command: provisions a new Mushi project, mints an API key with `mcp:read+write` scope, and writes `.env.local` + `.cursor/mcp.json`.

highlights.title: `mushi doctor`
highlights.description: pre-flight checks — verifies CLI config, endpoint reachability, SDK install, and optional server preflight.

highlights.title: `mushi nudge`
highlights.description: generates a paste-ready `Mushi.init()` snippet tuned for alpha / beta / ga release phases.

highlights.title: Rewards subsystem
highlights.description: (`src/rewards.ts`): full tester-rewards API — `initRewards`, `updateRewardsUser`, `getTier`, `enqueueActivity`. Connects to `/v1/rewards/*` backend endpoints.

highlights.title: Proactive triggers
highlights.description: (`src/proactive-triggers.ts`): schedule-based and event-based triggers that surface the widget at the right moment without developer code.

highlights.title: `pulseTrigger()`
highlights.description: widget-level pulse animation accessible from `Mushi.pulseTrigger()`.

highlights.title: Screenshot simplification
highlights.description: `screenshot.take()` now returns `Promise<string | null>` — callers no longer need to check an `{ ok, dataUrl }` result object.

highlights.title: INP (Interaction to Next Paint) capture
highlights.description: a Google Core Web Vital since March 2024, replacing First Input Delay. The SDK now installs a `PerformanceObserver({ type: 'event', durationThreshold: 40 })` and records the worst-observed user-interaction latency on every report, with **attribution** — `eventType`, `targetSelector` (e.g. `button#submit.primary`), and per-phase timings (input delay / processing / presentation) so the triage UI can render "1200 ms click on `<button.checkout>`" instead of a bare number. Falls back to a `first-input` observer for FID on Safari < 16.4. Adds ~700 B gzipped to the bundle (still under the 44 KB budget at 43.07 kB).

highlights.title: `beforeSendFeedback` hook
highlights.description: (Sentry SDK feedback spec §4): last-chance synchronous or async hook fired AFTER pre-filter / on-device classifier / rate-limit gates pass and BEFORE the report is sent or queued. Returning `null` drops the report silently; throwing or timing out (>2 s) ships the unmodified report so a buggy hook never swallows feedback.

highlights.title: `onCrashedLastRun` hook
highlights.description: (Sentry SDK feedback spec §6): fires once on `Mushi.init` after detecting that the previous tab session ended without a clean `pagehide`. The SDK never auto-opens the widget — copy and timing are the host's call. Implementation uses a `localStorage` sentinel that's set on init and cleared on `pagehide` (the only reliably-fired end-of-session event in 2026).

highlights.title: `createBreadcrumbBuffer(options?)`
highlights.description: — 50-entry ring buffer of `{ timestamp, category, level, message, data? }`. Long messages truncate at 500 chars _at insert time_ so a runaway log line can't push useful crumbs out of the buffer. PII scrubbing runs at report-snapshot time (not at insert) so in-app debugging stays unredacted while the wire payload stays clean.

highlights.title: `normaliseThrown(thrown)`
highlights.description: — turns any thrown value (`Error`, string, plain object, `null`, frozen `DOMException`) into `{ name, message, stack?, cause? }` with an 8 KB stack cap and cyclic-cause guards. Powers `Mushi.captureException()`; exposed so adapters (Vue / Svelte / Angular / RN) can ship their own thin wrappers without re-implementing the normalisation.

highlights.title: `captureEnvironment()` expansion
highlights.description: — same one-call API, richer payload: viewport + screen + DPR, **User-Agent Client Hints** when supported (`brands`, `mobile`, `platform`, `model`, plus the high-entropy values resolved out-of-band and folded back in on the next capture), accessibility prefs (`prefers-reduced-motion`, `prefers-color-scheme`, `prefers-contrast`), online / displayMode / page title, and a one-shot **page-load timing** read from `PerformanceNavigationTiming` (TTFB, DOMContentLoaded, FCP, LCP). Every individual field stays optional so a Safari / Firefox / iOS WebView still produces a useful payload.

highlights.title: `createBreadcrumbBuffer(options?)`

highlights.title: CLI
highlights.description: new `mushi skills` (`list`, `show`, `sync`) and `mushi pipeline` (`start`, `watch`, `checkin`) command groups. Browse the synced skill catalog, start a pipeline for a report, print the composed run packet, and check step progress back in from the terminal or CI.

highlights.title: MCP
highlights.description: five new tools so a Cursor agent can close the loop without leaving the IDE — `list_skills` and `get_skill` (read), plus `start_skill_pipeline`, `get_pipeline_run`, and `checkin_pipeline_step` (write). Each tool now advertises the correct title and `readOnlyHint` from the shared catalog so MCP clients render the right UI.

highlights.title: plugin-sdk
highlights.description: new `skill_pipeline.step.dispatched` event. Plugins can subscribe to react when a pipeline step is dispatched in cloud mode; the event payload carries `{ runId, stepIndex, skillSlug, contextPacket, projectId }`.

highlights.title: plugin-cursor-cloud
highlights.description: handles `skill_pipeline.step.dispatched` by running the step's pre-composed context packet as a Cursor Cloud agent, storing the agent run id on the step, and checking the step back in. Emits a clear warning when `MUSHI_API_KEY` or `repoUrl` is unset so dispatches can no longer fail silently.

highlights.description: new `skill_pipeline.step.dispatched` event. Plugins can subscribe to react when a pipeline step is dispatched in cloud mode; the event payload carries `{ runId, stepIndex, skillSlug, contextPacket, projectId }`.

highlights.title: plugin-cursor-cloud
highlights.description: handles `skill_pipeline.step.dispatched` by running the step's pre-composed context packet as a Cursor Cloud agent, storing the agent run id on the step, and checking the step back in. Emits a clear warning when `MUSHI_API_KEY` or `repoUrl` is unset so dispatches can no longer fail silently.

highlights.description: (`src/rewards.ts`): full tester-rewards API — `initRewards`, `updateRewardsUser`, `getTier`, `enqueueActivity`. Connects to `/v1/rewards/*` backend endpoints.

highlights.title: Proactive triggers
highlights.description: (`src/proactive-triggers.ts`): schedule-based and event-based triggers that surface the widget at the right moment without developer code.

highlights.title: `pulseTrigger()`
highlights.description: widget-level pulse animation accessible from `Mushi.pulseTrigger()`.

highlights.title: Screenshot simplification
highlights.description: `screenshot.take()` now returns `Promise<string | null>` — callers no longer need to check an `{ ok, dataUrl }` result object.

highlights.title: `beforeSendFeedback` + `onCrashedLastRun`
highlights.description: lifecycle hooks wired into the init flow.

highlights.title: `mushi project create`
highlights.description: command: provisions a new Mushi project, mints an API key with `mcp:read+write` scope, and writes `.env.local` + `.cursor/mcp.json`.

highlights.title: `mushi doctor`
highlights.description: pre-flight checks — verifies CLI config, endpoint reachability, SDK install, and optional server preflight.

highlights.title: `mushi nudge`
highlights.description: generates a paste-ready `Mushi.init()` snippet tuned for alpha / beta / ga release phases.

highlights.title: Fix scope fallback on typo
highlights.description: if `MUSHI_SCOPES` is set but contains only

highlights.title: XDG Base Directory compliance
highlights.description: config now lives at `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mushi/config.json` on Linux/macOS (defaulting to `~/.config/mushi/config.json`) and `%APPDATA%/mushi/config.json` on Windows. A pre-existing `~/.mushirc` is **automatically migrated on first load** — moved (not copied) into the new path so we never have two copies of the API key on disk simultaneously. Malformed legacy files are left in place for manual recovery rather than silently dropped. `loadConfig` now respects `MUSHI_API_KEY` / `MUSHI_PROJECT_ID` / `MUSHI_API_ENDPOINT` env vars even when no file exists, so CI pipelines work zero-config.

highlights.title: POSIX signal handling
highlights.description: SIGINT (Ctrl-C) and SIGTERM (`docker stop`, `kill <pid>`) are wired into a process-wide `AbortController`. Long-running commands (`mushi index`, `mushi sourcemaps upload`) now abort their in-flight HTTP requests immediately instead of hanging on the 15 s `apiCall` timeout. Exit codes follow POSIX convention: `130` for SIGINT, `143` for SIGTERM. Uses `AbortSignal.any` on Node ≥ 20 to compose with the per-request timeout signal.

highlights.title: Trackable error codes
highlights.description: every user-visible failure now flows through a structured `MushiCliError` carrying a stable `[E_*]` code (`E_AUTH_MISSING`, `E_NETWORK`, `E_RATE_LIMITED`, `E_INTERRUPTED`, etc.) and an actionable fix hint. Output format is grep-friendly:

highlights.title: Graph
highlights.description: — interactive ReactFlow canvas with layer-filter pills

highlights.title: Layer Sankey
highlights.description: — horizontal lane diagram showing files per architectural tier

highlights.title: Search
highlights.description: — semantic search via the `match_codebase_files` embedding RPC

highlights.title: Per-scope tool filtering
highlights.description: `createMushiServer({ scopes })` now restricts which tools appear in `tools/list`. A read-only API key (e.g. `MUSHI_SCOPES=mcp:read` env var on the stdio entry) hides every write tool, so the LLM never picks `dispatch_fix` only to receive an `INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE` 403 — round-trips and tokens saved on every interaction. Default behaviour (no `scopes` config) is unchanged: every tool is registered.

highlights.title: Output schemas + structuredContent (MCP 2025-06-18)
highlights.description: `get_recent_reports`, `search_reports`, `get_similar_bugs`, and `dispatch_fix` now declare an `outputSchema` and emit `structuredContent` alongside the existing text payload. Modern clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor 0.54+) read the typed object directly and pipe it into downstream tools without re-parsing JSON; older clients still see the text content.

highlights.title: MCP Inspector
highlights.description: `pnpm --filter @mushi-mushi/mcp inspector` now spawns the official `@modelcontextprotocol/inspector` against the local build for interactive tool exploration during development.

highlights.title: CLI
highlights.description: new `mushi skills` (`list`, `show`, `sync`) and `mushi pipeline` (`start`, `watch`, `checkin`) command groups. Browse the synced skill catalog, start a pipeline for a report, print the composed run packet, and check step progress back in from the terminal or CI.

highlights.title: MCP
highlights.description: five new tools so a Cursor agent can close the loop without leaving the IDE — `list_skills` and `get_skill` (read), plus `start_skill_pipeline`, `get_pipeline_run`, and `checkin_pipeline_step` (write). Each tool now advertises the correct title and `readOnlyHint` from the shared catalog so MCP clients render the right UI.

highlights.title: Community files in-tarball.
highlights.description: Snyk and Socket only credit community signals when the files are shipped inside the npm tarball, not when they live at the monorepo root. A pre-commit guard (`scripts/sync-community-files.mjs --check`) and the `pnpm release` script now auto-sync from the canonical root copies to prevent drift.

highlights.title: `publishConfig.provenance: true` everywhere.
highlights.description: The Release workflow already set `NPM_CONFIG_PROVENANCE=true` at the job level, but per-package `publishConfig` is the explicit signal Socket reads for its Supply Chain score. `@mushi-mushi/cli`, `create-mushi-mushi`, and `mushi-mushi` already had it; the remaining 20 publishable packages now match.

highlights.description: points at GitHub Sponsors so the repo exposes a funding signal to scanners and the GitHub UI.

highlights.title: `@mushi-mushi/cli` `0.3.0`
highlights.description: New `mushi init` command — interactive wizard built on `@clack/prompts` that auto-detects framework (Next, Nuxt, SvelteKit, Angular, Expo, Capacitor, plain React/Vue/Svelte, vanilla), package manager (npm/pnpm/yarn/bun), installs the right SDK, writes env vars with the right prefix (`NEXT_PUBLIC_`, `NUXT_PUBLIC_`, `VITE_`), warns when `.env.local` isn't gitignored, and prints the framework-specific snippet. Idempotent: never overwrites existing `MUSHI_*` env vars. Exposes new `./init` and `./detect` subpath exports for downstream packages.

highlights.title: `mushi-mushi` `0.3.0` (NEW, unscoped)
highlights.description: One-command launcher — `npx mushi-mushi` runs the wizard. Gives the SDK a single brand entry point on npm so users don't have to know to look under `@mushi-mushi/*` first.

highlights.title: `create-mushi-mushi` `0.3.0` (NEW)
highlights.description: `npm create mushi-mushi` — same wizard via the standard npm-create convention.

highlights.title: All 16 published packages
highlights.description: keyword sweep — every package now ships `mushi-mushi` plus its framework-specific terms (`react`, `next.js`, `vue`, `nuxt`, `svelte`, `sveltekit`, `angular`, `react-native`, `expo`, `capacitor`, `ionic`, etc.) plus product terms (`session-replay`, `screenshot`, `shake-to-report`, `sentry-companion`, `error-tracking`, `ai-triage`) for npm search ranking.

highlights.title: All SDK READMEs
highlights.description: discoverability cross-link header at the top — points users to the wizard and to every other framework SDK so people who land on `@mushi-mushi/react` can find `@mushi-mushi/vue` and vice-versa.

highlights.title: Root README
highlights.description: quick-start now leads with `npx mushi-mushi`, with the manual install path documented as the fallback. Packages table gains a row for the launcher.

highlights.title: npm keyword arrays expanded across all 14 publishable packages.
